stagedOutputChain.label: Windows staged-output chain
stagedOutputChain.summary: Directory verification, output-manifest staging, and checksum-manifest staging stay chained as one local-only Windows handoff before bundle sealing can be reviewed.

steps.label: Directory verification handoff
steps.summary: Verification metadata is ready so the staged-output lane starts from an explicit packaged-app root review checkpoint.

steps.label: Output manifest staging
steps.summary: The output manifest is the current Windows review focus so the staged-output handoff stays readable without producing a real packaged bundle.

steps.label: Checksum manifest staging
steps.summary: Checksum metadata remains chained behind the same staged-output task so output proof and digest posture stay in one lane.

reviewPacket.label: Windows local review packet
reviewPacket.summary: Windows keeps directory verification, staged-output manifests, and bundle-seal posture in one local-only review packet so the current handoff can be inspected without materializing anything.

steps.label: Directory -> staged-output handoff
steps.summary: Windows directory verification is already linked to the first staged-output handoff, so output-manifest review can start from the same package-root baseline.

steps.label: Staged-output -> checksum handoff
steps.summary: Windows output-manifest review keeps checksum proof in the same lane so staged-output evidence stays readable before any seal path can advance.

steps.label: Checksum -> bundle-seal handoff
steps.summary: Windows checksum proof is staged for review, but the bundle-seal handoff stays blocked until the local-only packet stops being metadata-only.

bundleSealingReadiness.label: Windows bundle-sealing readiness
bundleSealingReadiness.summary: Seal and integrity manifests are declared, but the Windows seal handoff remains blocked until the staged-output chain stops being metadata-only and the publish gate remains review-only.
bundleSealingReadiness.currentCheckpoint: seal manifest declared / staged-output review still active

checkpoints.label: Seal manifest declared
checkpoints.detail: Bundle sealing stays local-only, but the seal manifest path is already explicit for review pickup.

checkpoints.label: Integrity manifest declared
checkpoints.detail: Integrity posture stays visible beside the same seal handoff without digest publication or attestation execution.

checkpoints.label: Staged-output review still active
checkpoints.detail: Windows bundle sealing stays behind the current staged-output review slice until the output and checksum handoff stop being metadata-only.

localMaterializationProgress.label: Windows local materialization progression
localMaterializationProgress.summary: Directory review is complete, staged-output review is current, and bundle sealing stays queued as the next local-only checkpoint.

segments.label: Directory materialization
segments.summary: Directory verification is already closed out, so Windows review starts from an explicit packaged-app root checkpoint.

segments.label: Staged-output handoff
segments.summary: Windows is currently inside the staged-output handoff, with output and checksum manifests carrying the active local-only review posture.

segments.label: Bundle-sealing readiness
segments.summary: Bundle sealing is the next downstream slice, but it stays blocked behind the current staged-output review and review-only publish gate.

validatorObservabilityBridge.label: Windows validator / observability bridge
validatorObservabilityBridge.summary: Windows keeps directory, staged-output, and bundle-sealing validation mapped onto the boundary, approval, and preview observability rows so validator-facing progression can be followed outside the Stage Explorer without opening execution.

readouts.label: Directory continuity validator
readouts.summary: Windows directory verification is already visible on the boundary intake row, so main-shell review can re-read packaged roots and verification evidence without leaving the local-only lane.

readouts.label: Staged-output continuity validator
readouts.summary: Windows staged-output review is the active validator slice, and the trace review row keeps output/checksum evidence aligned with the same local-only approval posture.

readouts.label: Bundle-sealing continuity validator
readouts.summary: Windows bundle-sealing readiness is already mirrored onto the preview lifecycle row so the next checkpoint stays visible while staged-output review remains active.

tasks.label: Directory materialization
tasks.summary: Launcher path, resources tree, and verification manifest are explicit for reviewer pickup before any packaged-app root is materialized.

tasks.label: Staged output readiness
tasks.summary: Output and checksum manifests stay attached to the same packaged-app lane so the reviewer can inspect the staged-output handoff without emitting any real bundle.

tasks.label: Bundle-sealing readiness
tasks.summary: Seal and integrity manifests are linked, but bundle sealing stays blocked until staged outputs stop being metadata-only and later publish gates become executable.

steps.label: Checksum -> bundle-seal handoff
